description: The HR analytics team has accumulated 12,000 employee performance reviews in the PERF_REVIEWS table (owned by HR schema, credentials hr/hr123), all with STATUS='NEW'. These must be batch-processed nightly by a production-grade PL/SQL ETL pipeline that avoids row-by-row processing overhead. Implement a PL/SQL package named HR.REVIEW_ETL_PKG with a specification and body. The body must include a procedure PROCESS_REVIEWS that uses BULK COLLECT with a LIMIT clause to process rows in batches, computing a NORMALIZED_SCORE as ROUND((raw_score / max_possible_score) * 100, 2), updating STATUS to 'PROCESSED', and logging summary entries to the ETL_RUN_LOG table via an autonomous transaction sub-procedure LOG_ETL_RUN (declared with PRAGMA AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION). The package must also declare a weakly-typed REF CURSOR type and a function GET_UNPROCESSED returning a REF CURSOR over unprocessed reviews. Process all 12,000 rows using this package. Create a view HR.VW_REVIEW_SUMMARY joining PERF_REVIEWS with the DEPARTMENTS table to aggregate processed reviews by department, showing department_name, review_count, average normalized_score, and the reviewer_id with the highest review count. Export the view contents to /home/ga/Documents/exports/review_summary.csv. System credentials: system/OraclePassword123.

metadata.industry: Computer Systems Design and Related Services
